chapter_title.en: Chapter on Supplications at the Time of Mounting

text: 2418 - كَانَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «إِذَا وَضَعَ رِجْلَهُ فِي اَلرِّكَابِ يَقُولُ: «۝ سُبْحٰانَ اَلَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنٰا هٰذٰا وَ مٰا كُنّٰا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ ۝» وَ يُسَبِّحُ اَللَّهَ سَبْعاً وَ يُحَمِّدُ اَللَّهَ سَبْعاً وَ يُهَلِّلُ اَللَّهَ سَبْعاً».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2418 - When Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) placed his foot in the stirrup, he would say:
"Glory be to the One who has subjected this to us, and we could not have done so on our own." (Surah Az-Zukhruf 43:13)
Imam (as) would then glorify Allah (swt) seven times, praise Allah (swt) seven times, and declare Allah’s (swt) oneness seven times.
